name: AI Fruit / Object Drama
description: Pixar-3D-cartoon mini-soap-opera between anthropomorphic fruits / objects / animals with on-camera dialogue in 8s beats. 7×8s = 56s default. The format popularized by @fruittalesco — a melodramatic plot (affair, breakup, found-family) carried by direct character dialogue, not narrator. Veo 3.1 with native audio generates lip-synced speech directly; ElevenLabs adds NO additional music bed (Veo already injects ambient under each clip — two layers = mud). Single-word pop-up captions in TheBoldFont sit on top via Remotion.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — one keyframe per scene; first scene generates a character lineup, subsequent keyframes reuse the same cast description for consistency
stackSummary.i2v: google/veo-3.1 via OpenRouter — 8s clips, --audio ENABLED (Veo speaks the dialogue on-camera), --first-frame = scene keyframe. EN audio is clean; RU works but is warned in MODELS.md.
stackSummary.voiceover: NONE separately — Veo native audio IS the voiceover. Do not call ElevenLabs voiceover. Do not stack TTS on top.
stackSummary.music: NONE separately — Veo native audio already includes scene-appropriate ambient/score. Do not call ElevenLabs Music; layering it on top creates double-music mud. The 'no background music' anti-instruction in every Veo prompt is load-bearing.
stackSummary.captions: ElevenLabs scribe_v1 word-level → PopWordCaptions (TheBoldFont 700, one word at a time, spring pop-in)
stackSummary.composition: Remotion 4.0.441 — single OffthreadVideo of concat.mp4 (Veo audio preserved) + PopWordCaptions overlay

constants.vibeAnchor: fruittalesco — Pixar-3D anthropomorphic cast, melodramatic on-camera dialogue, soap-opera plot arc
constants.characterStyle: Pixar-3D cartoon, smooth glossy textures, big expressive eyes, simple humanoid clothing on a fruit/object/animal head, no photoreal humans
constants.captionStyle: PopWordCaptions — TheBoldFont 700, one word at a time, white with heavy black stroke + drop shadow, spring scale-in
constants.musicPolicy: Veo native audio only. NO ElevenLabs music. Every Veo prompt must include the no-music anti-instruction (see fragments.md → 'No-music clause').

variationAxes: cast type (fruits, vegetables, objects, animals, food items)
plot trope (affair / breakup / found-family / love-triangle / parent-child / mistaken-identity / glow-up)
tone (melodrama played straight, deadpan-comedy, soap-opera serious)
VO language (English by default — Veo native EN audio is the validated-clean path; Russian works but warned per MODELS.md; Chinese also clean)
doNotCopyLiterally: Scenario, dialogue lines, exact prompts, character cast. This is a vibe reference; the scenarist writes a fresh scenario every project. Per project you DO inherit the Veo-only-audio rule and the PopWordCaptions style.
referenceNotes: No user reference required. Cast is generated from prompt lock-strings. If the user names a real person or branded character, escalate to AGENTS.md hard rule #3 (reference-required gate).
